evals.prompt: i need to get all users who signed up in the last 7 days from clerk's backend API. show me how
evals.expected_output: REST API call to GET /v1/users with a millisecond created_at_after filter, Bearer token auth using CLERK_SECRET_KEY, response parsing from data with totalCount, and pagination handling
expectations: Uses GET /v1/users endpoint
Includes a millisecond timestamp query parameter such as created_at_after to narrow to the last 7 days
Sets CLERK_SECRET_KEY as Bearer token in the Authorization header
Parses users from the response data field and reads totalCount
Handles or mentions pagination via limit and offset parameters

evals.prompt: use the clerk backend api to create a new organization called 'Acme Corp' and invite user@example.com as an admin
evals.expected_output: Two API calls: POST /v1/organizations to create org, then POST /v1/organizations/{id}/invitations to invite with admin role
expectations: Uses POST /v1/organizations to create the organization with name 'Acme Corp'
Uses POST /v1/organizations/{id}/invitations to send the invitation
Sets the role to admin (or org:admin) in the invitation payload
Authenticates both API calls using CLERK_SECRET_KEY in the Authorization header
Extracts the organization ID from the first response and uses it in the invitation request
Validates CLERK_SECRET_KEY and checks the required write scopes before each POST
Prepares the exact request and requires explicit user confirmation before each POST is executed

evals.prompt: i need to set custom metadata on a user via clerk's backend api. set their plan to 'pro' and onboarded to true
evals.expected_output: PATCH /v1/users/{user_id} with public_metadata or private_metadata body, explanation of metadata types and when to use each
expectations: Uses PATCH /v1/users/{user_id} endpoint
Sets the metadata in the correct field: public_metadata, private_metadata, or unsafe_metadata
Explains the difference between metadata types (client-readable vs server-only vs client-writable)
Includes a properly formed JSON body with the plan and onboarded fields
Validates CLERK_SECRET_KEY and checks the required write scopes before the PATCH
Prepares the exact request and requires explicit user confirmation before executing the PATCH

evals.prompt: remove a user with id user_abc123 from our clerk instance using the backend api
evals.expected_output: DELETE /v1/users/{user_id} call with warning about irreversibility, CLERK_BAPI_SCOPES check, and CLERK_SECRET_KEY auth
expectations: Uses DELETE /v1/users/{user_id} endpoint with the correct user ID
Warns that the action is destructive and irreversible
Warns exactly which user data, sessions, memberships, and associated records will be irreversibly destroyed
Requires explicit confirmation before executing the delete
Includes CLERK_SECRET_KEY as Bearer token in the Authorization header
Validates CLERK_SECRET_KEY and checks the required write and delete scopes before execution
Mentions implications such as loss of user data, sessions, and associated records

evals.prompt: i'm updating user metadata to add a 'plan' field but it keeps deleting the existing 'role' field. what am i doing wrong?
evals.expected_output: PATCH metadata updates deep-merge existing metadata; PUT replaces the metadata object.
expectations: Explains that PATCH /v1/users/{user_id}/metadata deep-merges existing metadata
Uses updateUserMetadata(userId) for the SDK equivalent
Shows adding the plan field without manually replacing unrelated metadata
Explains that PUT /v1/users/{user_id}/metadata replaces the metadata object
States that the merge behavior applies to publicMetadata, privateMetadata, and unsafeMetadata

evals.prompt: my clerk backend api calls are failing with 429 errors in development. what are the rate limits?
evals.expected_output: Development has 100 req/10s limit (vs 1000/10s in production). Batch operations or reduce call frequency.
expectations: States the development rate limit (100 requests per 10 seconds)
States the production rate limit (1000 requests per 10 seconds)
Suggests batching operations or reducing call frequency
Mentions that currentUser() counts as an API call against the limit
Recommends using auth() instead of currentUser() when only session claims are needed
